name: North Dakota Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Examiners on Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
provider.parentOrganization: North Dakota Board of Examiners on Audiology and SLP

address.streetAddress: 3100 S. Columbia Rd., Suite 218, Grand Forks, ND 58201
address.description:
North Dakota Board of Examiners on Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
PO Box 5475
Bismarck, ND 58502-5475

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a North Dakota SLP license cost?
Is North Dakota part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does North Dakota allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with a valid ND license.

mainEntity.name: Does North Dakota have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. North Dakota participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in North Dakota without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to North Dakota?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in North Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
